explanations.2: SERIAL means the database automatically generates a unique number for each new region, starting from 1.
explanations.7: CHARACTER (2) means exactly 2 characters, unlike CHARACTER VARYING which allows up to that length—useful here because country codes are always 2 letters.
explanations.10: ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE means if a region changes or is deleted, all countries linked to it will automatically update or delete too.
explanations.26: location_id doesn't have NOT NULL, so a department can exist without being assigned to a location yet.
explanations.33: NUMERIC (8, 2) means the number can have up to 8 total digits with 2 after the decimal point, like 123456.78.
explanations.46: manager_id references employees back to itself, allowing each employee to have a manager who is also an employee.
explanations.50: This line creates a self-referential relationship—an employee can be managed by another employee in the same table.
